name: Corn Tortillas
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Finely ground deep yellow maize flour (masa harina)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the masa harina, cold water, and salt. Stir until smooth.
The dough should be slightly sticky and form a ball when pressed together.
2. To test the dough consistency, flatten a small piece between your palms.
If the edges crack, add water to the dough, one tablespoon at a time,
until a test piece does not crack.
3. Divide the dough into 24 golf ball-sized pieces for tacos and 12 large balls for quesadillas.
Place on a platter and cover with a damp towel.
4. Line a tortilla press with two sheets of plastic cut from a plastic freezer bag or other heavy-duty plastic bag.
5. Heat a dry cast iron skillet, nonstick pan, or comal until moderately hot.
6. Flatten each ball of dough in the tortilla press, then remove the top plastic sheet.
Holding the tortilla with your fingertips, peel off the bottom sheet and place it on the heated skillet.
Cook for about 30 to 45 seconds per side, pressing the top of each tortilla with your fingertips to make it puff.
7. Place the hot tortillas on a towel. When they are still warm but not hot, stack and wrap in a towel.
Serve immediately or let cool, wrap well in plastic or a plastic bag, and store in the refrigerator up to one week.
